title: Modes of opening file in C

content.problemStatement: Q5) A . Explain different modes of opening a file in C with their meanings ?   ---4 Marks.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. If the line is missing or empty, assume T = 1.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print the following 14 lines in the exact order, each on its own line:
 r: Read a file.
 w: Write a file.
 a: Append to a file.
 r+: Read and write a file.
 w+: Write and read a file.
 a+: Append and read a file.
 b: Binary mode.
 t: Text mode.
 rb: Read a binary file.
 wb: Write a binary file.
 ab: Append to a binary file.
 r+b: Read and write a binary file.
 w+b: Write and read a binary file.
 a+b: Append and read a binary file.
Separate consecutive test case outputs with a blank line.

r: Read a file.
w: Write a file.
a: Append to a file.
r+: Read and write a file.
w+: Write and read a file.
a+: Append and read a file.
b: Binary mode.
t: Text mode.
rb: Read a binary file.
wb: Write a binary file.
ab: Append to a binary file.
r+b: Read and write a binary file.
w+b: Write and read a binary file.
a+b: Append and read a binary file.
sampleTestCases.explanation: With no input, the program assumes T = 1 and prints the 14 lines once.

solution.approach: The task does not require any computation – we only have to repeat a fixed list of 14 descriptive strings for each test case. Read T (default 1 if missing), store the 14 lines in an array, and for each test case output them in order. Insert an empty line between test cases, but not after the last one. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – we print a constant 14 lines per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only the fixed array of 14 strings is stored.
